description_long: This object is a vintage Minolta X-370 film camera, featuring a classic rectangular body with a textured black grip and metallic silver top and bottom plates. The front displays the Minolta branding above the lens mount, with a prominent lens attached, complete with focus and aperture rings marked with numbers. The back of the camera has a small information plate detailing ISO settings and manufacturer information, as well as a viewfinder window. The top of the camera includes a film advance lever, shutter button, and a rewind knob, all rendered in metallic finishes. The lens is capped, and the overall design is robust, with visible screws and mechanical dials. The camera's form factor is boxy with rounded edges, typical of analog SLR cameras from the late 20th century.
description: A vintage Minolta X-370 film camera with a black textured grip, silver metallic top, and a capped lens, featuring mechanical dials and classic SLR styling.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the textured black grip, ISO information plate, and the viewfinder window.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the film advance lever, part of the lens barrel, and the camera's grip texture.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the Minolta logo, X-370 model badge, capped lens, and the main control dials.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the lens barrel, grip, and the shutter button area.
